rows.subject: E_AMMO (Ammo): custom touch
rows.notes: +5 with one-time hint; consumed by shooting

rows.subject: E_BOARD_EDGE (unnamed): custom touch
rows.notes: refusal with no neighbor; transfer with a neighbor board

rows.subject: E_BREAKABLE (Breakable): default draw/tick/touch
rows.notes: inert on touch; destroyed by a bullet

rows.subject: E_DOOR (Door): custom touch
rows.notes: matching key opens; missing key refuses

rows.subject: E_ENERGIZER (Energizer): custom touch
rows.notes: pickup/warning melodies and post-expiry state; no checkpoint while energized (flash color derives from vanilla-randomized CurrentTick the engine pins — scenario-design exclusion)

rows.subject: E_FAKE (Fake): custom touch
rows.notes: walk-through with one-time message

rows.subject: E_FOREST (Forest): custom touch
rows.notes: clears with message and sound

rows.subject: E_GEM (Gem): custom touch
rows.notes: counters via oracle sidebar

rows.subject: E_INVISIBLE (Invisible): custom touch
rows.notes: hidden in the initial capture, revealed to a normal wall on touch

rows.subject: E_KEY (Key): custom touch
rows.notes: pickup and duplicate-key refusal

rows.subject: E_LINE (Line): custom draw
rows.notes: neighbor-joined line characters compared in the board captures

rows.subject: E_MESSAGE_TIMER (unnamed): custom tick
rows.notes: message text, position, flash phase (P2), and expiry compared against the oracle

rows.subject: E_MONITOR (Monitor): custom tick
rows.notes: title-screen board state via every sweep scenario title checkpoint; exit-key set pinned by the unit test

rows.subject: E_NORMAL (Normal): default draw/tick/touch
rows.notes: touch refusal; bullet dies against it unharmed

rows.subject: E_PLAYER (Player): custom tick
rows.notes: also subject to collision-pushout and friendly-fire-policy; single-player V behavior is exact

rows.subject: E_RICOCHET (Ricochet): default draw/tick/touch
rows.notes: reversal proven with the shooter stepped aside; self-shot damage is the friendly-fire deviation, pinned by the M16.5 bullet row

rows.subject: E_TEXT_BLUE (unnamed): default draw/tick/touch
rows.notes: text tiles are drawn by the special case in game.go TileToColorAndChar, not a DrawProc

rows.subject: E_TEXT_CYAN (unnamed): default draw/tick/touch
rows.notes: text tiles are drawn by the special case in game.go TileToColorAndChar, not a DrawProc

rows.subject: E_TEXT_GREEN (unnamed): default draw/tick/touch
rows.notes: text tiles are drawn by the special case in game.go TileToColorAndChar, not a DrawProc

rows.subject: E_TEXT_PURPLE (unnamed): default draw/tick/touch
rows.notes: text tiles are drawn by the special case in game.go TileToColorAndChar, not a DrawProc

rows.subject: E_TEXT_RED (unnamed): default draw/tick/touch
rows.notes: text tiles are drawn by the special case in game.go TileToColorAndChar, not a DrawProc

rows.subject: E_TEXT_WHITE (unnamed): default draw/tick/touch
rows.notes: text tiles are drawn by the special case in game.go TileToColorAndChar, not a DrawProc

rows.subject: E_TEXT_YELLOW (unnamed): default draw/tick/touch
rows.notes: text tiles are drawn by the special case in game.go TileToColorAndChar, not a DrawProc

rows.subject: E_TORCH (Torch): custom touch
rows.notes: lit-room refusal, no-torch refusal, dark pickup, lighting circle, expiry

rows.subject: E_WATER (Water): custom touch
rows.notes: blocks with message

rows.subject: ZZT-OOP # command prefix vs bare text

rows.subject: Title screen mode (animated title board + menu)
Google OIDC sign-in vs guest identity

deviations.title: Death is a respawn (penalty + brief invulnerability), not game-over
Overlapping players: the arriver is pushed to a free adjacent square
Multiplayer projectile/contact damage follows an explicit friendly-fire policy
Modals freeze only the acting player; the room keeps ticking
World.Info.Flags are shared across players (co-op puzzle progress)
A restored snapshot drops other players; a joiner arrives fresh
Per-player keys/inventory live in an account sidecar, not the snapshot
The menu omits vanilla's S game-speed — the server owns the tick
A high score is entered on quit, not on death
Movement is arrows + numpad 8/4/6/2 only; WASD removed (S collides with save)
Pickup/shot/damage sounds are per-acting-player; #play stays room-wide
A windowed scroll is consumed on reply, not on touch (de-modal)
Presentation-only additions with no vanilla counterpart (name popup, picker, overlay, chat, sound UI, Dream, help/debug windows)
Mobile ships text entry only; touch gameplay is a filed gap task (M16.18a)
